vocab.meaning: mọt sách (người dành rất nhiều thời gian để đọc)
vocab.example: I've been a total bookworm since I was a toddler, always having my nose buried in a story.

vocab.meaning: hiểu ý tại ngôn ngoại (hiểu những ẩn ý không được viết trực tiếp)
vocab.example: I love complex psychological thrillers that require you to read between the lines to uncover the truth.

vocab.meaning: vùi đầu vào học tập/đọc sách một cách nghiêm túc
vocab.example: Whenever I want to master a new skill, I hit the books and research the topic as thoroughly as possible.

vocab.word: read someone like an open book
vocab.meaning: hiểu thấu tâm tư của ai đó (nhờ đọc nhiều nên hiểu tâm lý)
vocab.example: Reading so much classic literature has helped me understand people better; I can often read others like an open book.
The non-fiction books I read on sociology always provide plenty of food for thought regarding modern society.
Discussing a shared favorite novel with my professor definitely helped me get in her good books.

vocab.meaning: một người hoặc một thứ gì đó rõ ràng, không có bí mật
vocab.example: To me, science books are an open book that explains the mysteries of the universe in a clear way.

vocab.word: judge a book by its cover
vocab.meaning: đánh giá sự vật qua vẻ bề ngoài
vocab.example: I've learned not to judge a book by its cover; some of the most profound stories I've read had very plain designs.

vocab.word: the oldest trick in the book
vocab.meaning: chiêu trò cũ rích (thường dùng khi nói về các mô-típ truyện)
vocab.example: I've read so many mystery novels that I can spot the oldest trick in the book from a mile away.
While I usually follow the rules by the book, reading fantasy allows me to explore worlds where anything is possible.
